mechanism_narrative: A low and stable federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the cost of capital and supports higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below its 5-year average and stable.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a severe deterioration in the labor market and broader economic activity, leading to a sharp decline in corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by over 0.5pp MoM.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained strong retail sales growth combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates an overheating economy, which typically precedes aggressive monetary tightening and a subsequent economic slowdown, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% MoM AND FEDFUNDS has increased by > 0.25% over the last 3 months
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive on average when both retail sales growth is strong and FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: During periods following an economic contraction, an acceleration in industrial production signals a robust recovery in manufacturing and overall economic activity, which translates into improving corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In post-recession recovery regimes, periods of sustained INDPRO growth do not lead to significantly positive S&P 500 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, sudden decline in housing starts indicates a significant loss of confidence in the economic outlook among consumers and businesses, signaling an imminent broader economic contraction that negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 15% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are positive on average following a >15% MoM decline in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent and positive month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls reflects a healthy and expanding labor market, which underpins consumer spending and corporate profitability, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows consistent positive growth.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high and accelerating consumer price inflation with stagnant or declining GDP growth (stagflation) creates a challenging environment for corporate profitability and consumer purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth > 4% AND GDPC1 year-over-year growth < 1%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive on average during periods characterized by both high inflation and low GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of established economic expansion, robust year-over-year GDP growth confirms strong underlying economic momentum, which typically translates into strong corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: In periods of mature economic expansion, SPX 5-day returns are not significantly positive following periods of high GDPC1 growth.
